scope: Adult primary care threshold-focused referral support

immediate_emergency: Suspected torsion/rupture/haemorrhage with acute severe pain -> same-day emergency gynaecology assessment.
suspected_cancer_usc: USC if pelvic/abdominal mass on exam not clearly benign uterine/GI/urological.
USC if CA125 >=35 IU/ml with concerning symptoms and/or suspicious imaging.
USC if ultrasound suggests malignant features (solid/papillary/multiloculated with ascites etc.).

premenopausal_thresholds.simple_5to7cm: Repeat TVUS (typically 6-monthly, up to 12 months) and refer if persistent/enlarging.
premenopausal_thresholds.complex_any_size: CA125; if <40 years also LDH/AFP/hCG when non-simple; refer based on risk features.
premenopausal_thresholds.rmi_threshold: If RMI >200 (local pathways) -> USOC/oncology MDT route.

postmenopausal_thresholds.simple_le3cm: No routine follow-up (updated RCOG-consistent local guidance).
postmenopausal_thresholds.simple_3to5cm_with_normal_ca125: Repeat TVUS+CA125 at 6-month intervals up to 1 year; discharge if stable/reducing.
postmenopausal_thresholds.non_simple_any_size: Refer with CA125 and RMI calculation.
postmenopausal_thresholds.rmi_threshold: RMI >200 (local) or >=250 (NICE secondary care recommendation) indicates high-risk escalation to specialist MDT.

investigation_basics: TVUS is first-line imaging where feasible (higher sensitivity than TAUS).
CA125 should not be used alone to exclude ovarian cancer.
If symptoms persist with non-diagnostic tests, safety-net and reassess promptly.

rationale: Strong agreement across NICE + Scottish referral guideline + multiple territorial pathways + RCOG.
Minor threshold variance for RMI (200 local operational vs 250 NICE specialist referral).
Some sources had technical extraction/access limits (CKS access, PDF parsing), but key thresholds were corroborated by multiple independent sources.
